原文: Many people think that positive thinking① is mostly about keeping one's head in the sand and ignoring daily problems, trying to look optimistic②. In reality it has more to do with the way an individual③ talks to himself. Self-talk④ is a constant stream of thoughts of a person, who is often unaware⑤ and uncertain⑥ of some events, phenomena⑦, people, or even the person himself. B. Negative self-talk damages self-confidence⑧ and decreases self-respect⑨. Meanwhile, positive thinking can help to stop negative self-talks and start to form a positive view on an issue⑩. People who regularly practise positive thinking tend to solve problems more effectively⑪. They are less exposed to stress⑫ caused by external factors⑬. They tend to believe in themselves and in what they do.
翻译: 许多人认为积极思考主要是关于逃避现实、忽视日常问题，试图表现得乐观。实际上，它更多地与一个人自我对话的方式有关。自我对话是一个人持续不断的想法流，这个人常常对一些事件、现象、他人，甚至对自己本人没有意识到或不确定。消极的自我对话会损害自信心并降低自尊。同时，积极思考可以帮助停止消极的自我对话，并开始对问题形成积极的看法。经常练习积极思考的人往往能更有效地解决问题。他们较少受到外部因素引起的压力的影响。他们倾向于相信自己和自己所做的事情。

原文: D. Positive thinking has several beneficial⑭ effects on the body and the mind. People who think positively demonstrate increased life spans (寿命), lower rates of depression⑮ and anxiety⑯, better physical and psychological⑰ health, reduced risks of death from heart problems. Positive thinking also contributes to one's ability to deal with problems and hardships⑱. C. It helps one to remain clear-headed⑲ and confident in difficult situations. For example, researchers have found that in the case of a crisis⑳ accompanied by strong emotions, such as a natural disaster, positive thinking can provide a sort of buffer (缓冲作用) against depression and anxiety. Resilient (适应性强的) people who think positively tend to treat every problem as a challenge, a chance for improvement of any kind, or as an opportunity for personal growth. Pessimists, on the contrary, tend to perceive problems as a source of additional stress. G. They often feel discouraged long before trying to solve the problem, even if small.
翻译: 积极思考对身体和心灵有几个有益的影响。积极思考的人表现出更长的寿命、更低的抑郁和焦虑率、更好的身体和心理健康、降低因心脏问题死亡的风险。积极思考也有助于一个人处理问题和困难的能力。它帮助一个人在困难情况下保持头脑清醒和自信。例如，研究人员发现，在伴随强烈情绪（如自然灾害）的危机情况下，积极思考可以提供一种对抗抑郁和焦虑的缓冲作用。适应性强的积极思考者倾向于将每个问题视为挑战、任何形式的改进机会或个人成长的机会。相反，悲观主义者倾向于将问题视为额外压力的来源。他们常常在尝试解决问题之前很久就感到气馁，即使问题很小。
必背词汇: ⑭ beneficial /ˌbenɪˈfɪʃl/ adj. 有益的，有利的 Ⓡ benefit /ˈbenɪfɪt/ n. 益处 v. 受益 Ⓡ beneficiary /ˌbenɪˈfɪʃəri/ n. 受益人 Ⓡ beneficially /ˌbenɪˈfɪʃəli/ adv. 有益地 Ⓡ benefactor /ˈbenɪfæktə(r)/ n. 捐助者 ⑮ depression /dɪˈpreʃn/ n. 抑郁；萧条 Ⓡ depress /dɪˈpres/ v. 使沮丧；使萧条 Ⓡ depressed /dɪˈprest/ adj. 沮丧的；萧条的 Ⓡ depressing /dɪˈpresɪŋ/ adj. 令人沮丧的 Ⓡ depressive /dɪˈpresɪv/ adj. 抑郁的 ⑯ anxiety /æŋˈzaɪəti/ n. 焦虑；渴望 Ⓡ anxious /ˈæŋkʃəs/ adj. 焦虑的；渴望的 Ⓡ anxiously /ˈæŋkʃəsli/ adv. 焦虑地 Ⓡ anxiety disorder 焦虑症 Ⓡ anxiety attack 焦虑发作 ⑰ psychological /ˌsaɪkəˈlɒdʒɪkl/ adj. 心理的 Ⓡ psychology /saɪˈkɒlədʒi/ n. 心理学 Ⓡ psychologist /saɪˈkɒlədʒɪst/ n. 心理学家 Ⓡ psychologically /ˌsaɪkəˈlɒdʒɪkli/ adv. 心理上 Ⓡ psychosomatic /ˌsaɪkəʊsəˈmætɪk/ adj. 心身的 ⑱ hardship /ˈhɑːdʃɪp/ n. 困难；困苦 Ⓡ hard /hɑːd/ adj. 困难的；硬的 Ⓡ harden /ˈhɑːdn/ v. 使变硬；使坚强 Ⓡ hardiness /ˈhɑːdinəs/ n. 耐劳；强壮 Ⓡ hard-hearted /ˌhɑːd ˈhɑːtɪd/ adj. 硬心肠的 ⑲ clear-headed /ˌklɪə ˈhedɪd/ adj. 头脑清醒的 Ⓡ clear /klɪə(r)/ adj. 清楚的 v. 清除 Ⓡ clearly /ˈklɪəli/ adv. 清楚地 Ⓡ clarity /ˈklærəti/ n. 清晰 Ⓡ clear up 清理；放晴 ⑳ crisis /ˈkraɪsɪs/ n. 危机；紧要关头 Ⓡ crises /ˈkraɪsiːz/ n. 危机（复数） Ⓡ critical /ˈkrɪtɪkl/ adj. 关键的；批评的 Ⓡ critic /ˈkrɪtɪk/ n. 批评家 Ⓡ criticize /ˈkrɪtɪsaɪz/ v. 批评

原文: In conclusion, positive thinking is a powerful and effective tool for dealing with hard times and improving the quality of one's life. It doesn't have anything to do with ignorant optimism when an individual refuses to notice a problem. A. It doesn't cause any severe emotional discomfort, either.
翻译: 总之，积极思考是应对困难时期和提高生活质量的一个强大而有效的工具。它与当一个人拒绝注意到问题时的无知乐观无关。它也不会引起任何严重的情感不适。

原文: Thinking in a positive, self-encouraging way brings about many benefits to one's physical and mental health.
翻译: 以积极、自我鼓励的方式思考会给一个人的身心健康带来许多益处。
